get.description: List all Users the authenticated user/authtoken has access to. The entries can be filtered by the provided query parameters, all of which are optional. The number of entries returned is capped by the page size limit. This end point uses cursor pagination. If more entries would be found for a given query, the result envelope contains a 'next_page_token' field with a token that can be used to fetch the next page.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: pass a user name to filter entries by the user name

parameters.name: entry_date_from
parameters.description: pass entry_date_from to show all users entered on or after defined day

parameters.name: entry_date_to
parameters.description: pass entry_date_from to show all users entered on or before defined day

parameters.name: resign_date_from
parameters.description: pass resign_date_from to show all users resigned on or after defined day

parameters.name: resign_date_to
parameters.description: pass resign_date_to to show all users resigned on or before defined day

parameters.name: resigned
parameters.description: pass parameter resigned true/false to show all users who are resigned/not resigned

parameters.name: employee_number
parameters.description: pass an employee_number to show users with the corresponding employee number

parameters.name: page_token
parameters.description: Token for retrieving the next page. This token is provided in the response of the previous page as next_page_token

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: pass a value to change the maximum number of entries returned in a single page (at max 500).

parameters.name: sort_direction
parameters.description: pass a direction in which the entries returned will be sorted in

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description:
By default sorted by:
 * Lastname
 * Firstname 
 * Id

pass a property by which the returned entries will be sorted if you want a different order

Link.description: link to the next page including filter parameters and page token

post.description: Creates a new User based on the data provided in the request body.

get.description: Retrieves a single User based on its User ID.

patch.description: Updates the User specified by the id with the fields provided in the request body.

get.summary: get a user's working time accounts
get.description: Retrieves a User's working time accounts balance to a given day.
Retrieves the corresponding Work-Schedule-Model with durations in minutes based on a User's ID

get.summary: Fetches all tasks the user is assigned to
get.description:
Lists all tasks the authenticated user/authtoken is permitted to see.

The response includes tasks regardless of whether the tasks are currently active or locked, since the API does not filter tasks based on the request time. If only active tasks should be displayed (as in the timr UI), this filtering must be implemented on the client side.

The entries can be filtered by the provided query parameters, all of which are optional. The number of entries returned is capped by the page size limit. This end point uses cursor pagination. If more entries would be found for a given query, the result envelope contains a 'next_page_token' field with a token that can be used to fetch the next page.

parameters.name: parent_task_id
parameters.description: pass task's id to restrict entries having this task as a parent task

parameters.name: traverse_task_tree
parameters.description: pass this parameter with value true/false to traverse the whole task tree

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: pass a user name to filter entries by the user name

parameters.name: bookable
parameters.description: pass this parameter with value true/false to show just entries/no entries which are billable

parameters.name: billable
parameters.description: pass this parameter with value true/false to show just entries/no entries which are billable

parameters.name: page_token
parameters.description: Token for retrieving the next page. This token is provided in the response of the previous page as next_page_token

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: pass a value to change the maximum number of entries returned in a single page (at max 500).

parameters.name: sort_direction
parameters.description: pass a direction in which the entries returned will be sorted in

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description:
By default sorted by:
 * breadcrumbs
 * id

pass a property by which the returned entries will be sorted if you want a different order

Link.description: link to the next page including filter parameters and page token

put.description: Updates the Track specified by the id of a Drive Log with the waypoints provided in the request body.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: drive log id

200.description: working time date span successfully updated

delete.description: Deletes a single Track based on a Drive Log's id.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: drive log id

204.description: Empty response indicating that delete was successful

get.description: List all Working Times Requests the authenticated user/authtoken has access to. The entries can be filtered by the provided query parameters, all of which are optional. The number of entries returned is capped by the page size limit. This end point uses cursor pagination. If more entries would be found for a given query, the result envelope contains a 'next_page_token' field with a token that can be used to fetch the next page.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: pass a date to filter entries started from this day on

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: pass a date to filter entries started up to (including) this day

parameters.name: last_modified_after
parameters.description: pass a datetime to filter entries modified after this instant

parameters.name: working_time_types
parameters.description: pass one or more ids of Working Time Types to restrict entries based on these types

parameters.name: statuses
parameters.description: pass one or more statuses to filter entries based on their status

parameters.name: users
parameters.description: pass one or more user ids to restrict entries based on the user they are associated to

parameters.name: substitutes
parameters.description: pass one or more user ids to restrict entries based on the substitute they are associated to

parameters.name: page_token
parameters.description: Token for retrieving the next page. This token is provided in the response of the previous page as next_page_token

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: pass a value to change the maximum number of entries returned in a single page (at max 500).

parameters.name: sort_direction
parameters.description: pass a direction in which the entries returned will be sorted in

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description:
By default sorted by:
 * start
 * Id

Link.description: link to the next page including filter parameters and page token

get.summary: gets a single Working Time Request
get.description: Retrieves a single Working Time Request based on its id.

delete.summary: deletes a single Working Time Request
delete.description: Deletes a single Working Time based on its id.

204.description: Empty response indicating that delete was successful

parameters.name: sort_direction

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description:
By default sorted by:
 * last_modified
 * Id
optionally, pass a property by which the returned entries will be sorted if you want a different order

Link.description: link to the next page including filter parameters and page token

post.summary: create a Working Time Date Span
post.description: Creates a new Working Time Date Span based on the data provided in the request body.

get.summary: gets a single Working Time Date Span
get.description: Retrieves a single Working Time Date Span based on its id.

200.description: corresponding working time date span entity for the given id

patch.summary: updates a single Working Time Date Span
patch.description: Updates the Working Time Date Span specified by the id with the fields provided in the request body.

200.description: working time date span successfully updated

204.description: Empty response indicating that a date span got deleted. This can happen when it is updated to a day where no working hours can be booked.

delete.summary: deletes a single Working Time Date Span
delete.description: Deletes a single Working Time Date Span based on its id.

204.description: Empty response indicating that delete was successful

minutes.description: Net minutes of the recording. In case of type 'half_day' or 'full_day' these get calculated automatically, based on WorkTimeSchedule configured for User

minutes_rounded.description: Net minutes rounded to the default setting configured. In case of type 'half_day' or 'full_day' these get calculated automatically, based on WorkTimeSchedule configured for User

duration_minutes.description: Duration of break in minutes. Please make sure it matches the duration between 'start' and 'end' if the properties are also given.

start.description: start and end are optional. To avoid discrepancies in the duration given in duration_minutes and the time between start and end the preferable way is to only provide duration_minutes.

end.description: start and end are optional. To avoid discrepancies in the duration given in duration_minutes and the time between start and end the preferable way is to only provide duration_minutes.

email.description: Email address must be either valid or null.

login.x-pattern-message: login may only contain alphanumeric characters, dashes, dots or @ symbols
login may only contain alphanumeric characters, dashes, dots or @ symbols

duration_actual_minutes.description: sum of durations in current period

balance_current_period_minutes.description: difference between target duration and actual duration

work_from_home_days_full.description: complete days worked from home in current period

work_from_home_days_partial.description: partial days worked from home in current period

normal_working_times.description: this property is always set, when the WorkScheduleModel is of type normal_working_time, in contrast when the WorkScheduleModel is of type daily_target_hours it is not set

duration_type.description: Must be specified for WorkingTimeTypes with `edit_unit` `days` or `half_days` only

budget_update_project_times.description: this property is used to decide whether existing project times that have been booked to this task should be updated with the changed budget.  If budget_planning type or budget_hourly_rate are set, this property must also be set.   Based on this, the budgets of the existing project times are then recalculated or not.

parent_task_id.description: returns the id of the immediate parent task if the task has one. Use this id when creating or a updating a task in order to locate it at the right place in your task tree.

bookable.description: when a task is not bookable, no project times can be booked on it. You need to define child tasks, to book on tasks related to this task.

project_time_notes_required.description: depending on this property project times booked on this task have to include notes or not

start_date.description: the task is only available for selection after start_date, however, it can change if a lock_date is set and this is after the start_date. Furthermore, no booking of a project time with date < start or > end is possible.

end_date.description: the task is only available for selection before end_date, however, it can change if a lock_date is set and this is before the end_date. Furthermore, no booking of a project time with date < start or > end is possible.

lock_date.description:
with lock_date property you define a point in time before which existing entries can no longer be changed. This way you ensure that

  * existing entries cannot be changed or deleted
  * no entries can be added later

earliest_start_time.description: set this property to limit the task so that it cannot be booked before this time

latest_end_time.description: set this property to limit the task so that it cannot be booked after this time

budget_inherited.description: when budget is inherited from parent you cannot set it manually. Set this property to false, if you want to change anything in the tasks budget settings

budget_planning_type.description: when no budget is planned, either don't set this property (on create) or set it to NONE

budget_hours_planned.description:
how many hours were planned in the calculation
set budget_hours_planned to make timr calculate budget_planned depending on its budget_planning_type:
  * task_hourly_rate: out of budget_hourly_rate and budget_planned
  * user_hourly_rate: out of fixed hours budget (employee budget) and budget_hours_planned
  * fixed_price: out of budget_hourly_rate and budget_planned

budget_hourly_rate.description:
depending on its budget_planning_type this property may or may not be set:
  * **task_hourly_rate: this property is necessary to calculate budgets, by default it will be set to 0.00**
  * **user_hourly_rate: this property cannot be set**
  * fixed_price: you can set this property if you want to make timr calculate budget_hours_planned or budget_planned based on its value

budget_planned.description:
the price at which the task was sold
set budget_planned to make timr calculate the budget_hours_planned depending on its budget_planning_type:
  * task_hourly_rate: out of budget_hourly_rate and budget_planned
  * user_hourly_rate: out of fixed hours budget (employee budget) and budget_planned
  * fixed_price: out of budget_hourly_rate and budget_planned

budget_include_non_billable_project_time.description: set this property to true, if you want to include not billable hours for all project times booked on this task

location_inherited.description: set this property to false if you want to set a child task's location explicitly instead of inheriting it from its parent

location_restriction_radius_meters.description: set this property to make recording of project times for this task via the app on the smartphone only possible within this defined geofence radius

custom_field_1.description: you can configure custom fields for tasks in the settings of timr under Administration/Settings/Tasks. To add a new custom field, simply choose a name for the field.

custom_field_2.description: you can configure custom fields for tasks in the settings of timr under Administration/Settings/Tasks. To add a new custom field, simply choose a name for the field.

custom_field_3.description: you can configure custom fields for tasks in the settings of timr under Administration/Settings/Tasks. To add a new custom field, simply choose a name for the field.

TaskLocation.description: You can define the location of a task by setting this property. If the task has a parent task, the location is inherited if nothing else is explicitly defined. If you set this property to null, the location will be deleted.

BudgetPlanningType.description:
corresponding to the chosen planning type some properties cannot be set:
  * none: no further budget properties can be set
  * user_hourly_rate: budget_hourly_rate cannot be set

for the remaining two types all budget properties are supported

WorkingTimeRequestPartial.description: References a request if the working time is part of one.  A working time can either be part of a working time request or a working time date span, never both.

WorkingTimeDateSpanPartial.description: References a date span if the working time is part of one.  A working time can either be part of a working time date span or a request, never both.

recording_mode_user.description: Specifies how the user can record this working time type. Only relevant for working time types having edit_unit 'half_days' or 'days', otherwise null

automatic_break_deduction_policy.description: Policy that defines how automatic break deductions are handled for this working time type. Only applicable for minute based attendance times (WorkingTimeTypeCategory = 'attendance_time' and edit_unit = 'minutes'). Defaults to 'deduction_allowed' if not set.
